flags.concern: video-editor-layout-transition: Step 2's proposed `transition-opacity`/`duration-200` classes on the conditional `<main>` branches will not animate the standard↔condensed swap, because `VideoEditorShell` renders those layouts as mutually exclusive subtrees. React will unmount one branch and mount the other immediately, so there is no opacity change for CSS to interpolate and the swap still lands as a hard cut unless the plan uses a real mount animation or keeps both layouts present during the transition.
flags.evidence: In `src/tools/video-editor/components/VideoEditorShell.tsx:360-452`, the shell uses `condensed ? <main ...> : <main ...>`. The plan's Step 2 only adds transition classes to those `<main>` nodes. A plain `transition-opacity` class does not animate mount/unmount by itself.

flags.concern: video-editor-layout-transition: The verification plan does not add any direct coverage for the behavior being changed. The repo has no `VideoEditorShell` tests, and the listed `LayoutMainContent` test only asserts padding-bottom, so the new ResizeObserver-driven condensed threshold and swap timing would still be validated only by manual smoke testing.
flags.evidence: A repo search only finds `VideoEditorShell` references in `src/app/components/LayoutMainContent.test.tsx`, and that suite checks layout container styles such as `paddingBottom` rather than `VideoEditorShell`'s `ResizeObserver` logic or `condensed` rendering.
